metadata.title: Pig Butchering (Sha Zhu Pan) Scam Detection Test Data
metadata.source: Compiled from Chainalysis, TRM Labs, FBI IC3, Elliptic, ProPublica, and multiple news sources
metadata.purpose: Training data for scam detection system

scam_overview.name: Pig Butchering (Sha Zhu Pan)
scam_overview.definition: Long-con investment fraud where criminals build fake romantic or business relationships over weeks/months, then 'slaughter' victims by draining their crypto wallets

known_fake_platforms.name: bigoneit.site
known_fake_platforms.notes: Fake version of legitimate crypto exchange

known_fake_platforms.name: UniApp
known_fake_platforms.notes: Distributed through official app stores and phishing sites

known_fake_platforms.name: Huione Guarantee
known_fake_platforms.notes: Used to support pig butchering industry and scam technology products

known_wallet_addresses.notes: Traced to individuals in Myanmar for $164K loss case
Blacklisted by Tether, incoming exposure from confirmed pig butchering wallets

initial_contact.description: Text from 'wrong number' that initiates conversation
Match on dating app who quickly moves conversation to messaging apps
Direct message from stranger on Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, X
Request to move conversation to encrypted messaging apps

romance_patterns.description: Excessive flattery, affection, and attention early in relationship
Never shows face on video, always has excuses
Uses stock photos, AI-generated images, or stolen photos
Shows expensive cars, luxury travel, designer goods

investment_patterns.description: Shows screenshots of large trading profits
Directs to unknown or fake trading platform
indicators: Platform URL looks similar to real exchange
Asks to download app outside app store

phrases: I'll show you exactly when to buy/sell

investment_patterns.description: Allows victim to withdraw small amounts initially to build trust
investment_patterns.purpose: Victim sees 'proof' that platform is legitimate

phrases: Need to pay fees to withdraw

indicators: URL slightly different from real exchange

initial_contact: Hi! Sorry, wrong number, but you seem nice...
I got your number from [friend's name]
I saw your profile and wanted to connect
relationship_building: What do you do for work?
I made good money in crypto recently
My uncle/mentor showed me this strategy
I can teach you if you're interested
investment_pitch: I'll show you exactly when to buy and sell
This platform has better rates than Coinbase
Download this app, it's more private
Send me your wallet address, I'll send you some to start
You need to act fast, market is moving
urgency_tactics: My mentor says this is the one
Market conditions are perfect right now
post_loss: You need to pay taxes to withdraw
Account frozen, need fee to release
I lost everything too, let's figure it out together
Customer support is working on it
Just one more deposit to fix this

test_cases.scenario: Text from unknown number saying 'Hey, is this Mike?' followed by 'Sorry wrong number but you seem interesting'
test_cases.expected_result: HIGH RISK - Classic pig butchering opener

test_cases.scenario: Match on Tinder who quickly moves to WhatsApp, avoids video calls, shows wealth photos, mentions crypto profits
test_cases.expected_result: CRITICAL RISK - Multiple pig butchering red flags

test_cases.scenario: Platform that looks like Binance but URL is binanace.com (typo), asks for direct wallet connection
Platform allows small $100 withdrawal to prove legitimacy, then demands fees for larger withdrawal
test_cases.expected_result: HIGH RISK - Pig butchering small win pattern

test_cases.scenario: Invitation to VIP trading group with 'pump signals' and 'guaranteed returns'
test_cases.expected_result: HIGH RISK - Telegram scam group pattern

test_cases.scenario: X/Twitter DM saying 'Impressed by your work! Let's collaborate!' from crypto account
Online relationship for weeks, person shows wealth, eventually mentions crypto profits and offers to teach trading
test_cases.expected_result: CRITICAL RISK - Classic romance + crypto pig butchering

test_cases.scenario: After investing, told need to pay 'taxes' or 'fees' before withdrawal

examples: Impressed by your work! Let's collaborate!
Hi! Ready to collaborate for a brighter future?
Awesome! Want free assistance? DM me anytime!
Hey there! Exploring opportunities for synergy?
Send a DM for more info

prevention_tips: Never respond to 'wrong number' texts from strangers
Be suspicious of anyone on dating apps who quickly moves conversation to other platforms
Never send money or crypto to someone you haven't met in person
Video call early - scammers avoid showing their face
If returns seem too good to be true, they are
Never pay 'fees' or 'taxes' to withdraw your own money
Report to FBI IC3, FTC, and local authorities
